description.task: Implement a TreeTraversals class with inorder, preorder, postorder (all recursive), and level-order (BFS using a queue) traversals. Each method returns the visited node values as a List<Integer>.
instructions: preorder: add root.val first, then recursively preorder left, then right
postorder: recursively postorder left, then right, then add root.val
levelOrder: while queue not empty: poll node, add val to result, enqueue left (if not null), enqueue right (if not null)
inorder is already implemented as reference
description.hint: preorder helper: result.add(node.val); preorderHelper(node.left,result); preorderHelper(node.right,result); levelOrder: while(!queue.isEmpty()){Node n=queue.poll(); result.add(n.val); if(n.left!=null)queue.add(n.left); if(n.right!=null)queue.add(n.right);}

description.task: Implement an IterativeTraversals class that performs inorder traversal iteratively using an explicit stack (no recursion). Also implement iterative preorder traversal.
instructions: inorderIterative: while curr!=null push curr, curr=curr.left; then curr=stack.pop(); result.add(curr.val); curr=curr.right
preorderIterative: pop node, add val; if right!=null push right; if left!=null push left (left pushed last so it pops first)
Both should produce the same output as recursive versions
Do not use any recursive calls

description.task: Implement a ZigzagTraversal class that traverses a binary tree level by level, alternating direction: left-to-right for even levels and right-to-left for odd levels. Return each level as a list.
instructions: For each level: poll 'size' nodes, enqueue their children; fill the 'level' int array using index i when leftToRight, or (size-1-i) when right-to-left
Do not use Collections.reverse(); use the index trick instead
description.hint: for(int i=0;i<size;i++){Node n=queue.poll(); int idx=leftToRight?i:size-1-i; level[idx]=n.val; if(n.left!=null)queue.add(n.left); if(n.right!=null)queue.add(n.right);}
